title: papaver rupifragum subsp. atlanticum
intro: Papaver rupifragum subsp. atlanticum, commonly known as the Atlantic Poppy, is a subspecies of the Papaver rupifragum, a perennial flowering plant native to the western Mediterranean region. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its alkaloid content, which has been used to treat various health issues. The Atlantic Poppy has been traditionally used to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain, offering potential health benefits in managing conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Herbal preparations of Papaver rupifragum subsp. atlanticum, often in the form of tinctures or teas, have been used to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
